name: watch
description: Let Claude watch a video — and fetch only the evidence your question needs. Ask a question and /watch retrieves the relevant chapters, pricing/benchmark lines, and on-screen moments instead of sampling the whole timeline: 60-80% fewer tokens with equal-or-better answers in blind paired judging. Fork of Bradley Bonanno's claude-video; same install, same defaults, evidence mode is opt-in.

interface.shortDescription: Give the agent a video input — ask a question, get only the evidence that answers it.
interface.longDescription: watch lets the agent watch any video: it downloads with yt-dlp, extracts frames with ffmpeg, pulls a timestamped transcript from native captions (or the Whisper API as a fallback), and hands frames + transcript to the model. This fork of Bradley Bonanno's claude-video adds question-aware evidence retrieval: pass your question and it selects whole topical chapters, rescues pricing/benchmark lines from elsewhere in the video, and grabs frames at chapter starts, 'as you can see' cue moments, and on-screen tables — 60-80% fewer tokens than whole-timeline sampling with equal-or-better answers in blind paired judging. Any failure falls back to the original pipeline automatically.
defaultPrompt: /watch https://youtu.be/<launch-video> what's actually new — skip the hype?
